name: trend-oracle
hypothesis: cand-0008 scored 0.975 but search-007 (notifications) and search-011 (url-shortener) needed 2 rounds and had DoD gaps (20/22 and 22/24). Analysis: both are multi-component hard tasks where the adversary found issues the planner didn't anticipate. By adding (1) trend-aware planning that front-loads historically-common failure patterns and (2) a structured adversary taxonomy ensuring systematic coverage across 4 failure categories, we should eliminate round-2 regressions on hard tasks and push those from A to A+.

changes.planner: Added trend analysis step: 5 historically-common failure patterns (concurrency, boundaries, type-safety, error-propagation, integration-seams). Each feature must document which patterns it covers via Trend Coverage and a Trend Risk Matrix.
changes.generator: Same as cand-0008 with added instruction to implement defensively based on trend risks in the plan.
changes.adversary: Restructured from ad-hoc probing to structured 4-category taxonomy (data-integrity, concurrency, boundary, error-propagation). Must provide ≥1 challenge per applicable category. Timeout increased 12→15 min for deeper structured analysis.
changes.evaluator: Same as cand-0008 with added per-criterion confidence tracking (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). A+ requires avg confidence ≥0.9.
changes.skill: Same workflow and timeouts except adversary 12→15 min.
